context: Evaluates implementation of Windows batch file argument escaping utilities used to safely execute MetaEditor commands via Wine. The candidate must correctly implement two different escaping strategies and combine them into batch file content.

checklist.name: Full batch argument quoting
checklist.description: escapeBatchArg wraps the value in double quotes and correctly escapes %, ", ^, and the special characters &, |, <, >, (, ), @, ! using the documented escaping rules

checklist.name: Metacharacter-only escaping
checklist.description: escapeBatchMeta escapes only % as %% and does not add outer quotes or escape other metacharacters, preserving existing quoting structures in arguments

checklist.name: Control character validation
checklist.description: Both escapeBatchArg and escapeBatchMeta throw an Error when the input contains \r, \n, or null bytes

checklist.name: Batch content structure
checklist.description: buildBatchContent produces content starting with '@echo off\r\n', uses escapeBatchArg for the executable and escapeBatchMeta for arguments, with CRLF line endings

checklist.name: Spaces in paths
checklist.description: The generated batch content correctly handles executable paths containing spaces (wrapped in double quotes by escapeBatchArg) so cmd.exe can parse them
